package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for admin_or_debug_endpoint_registration_mixed_into_public_router_setup
// Anti-pattern: Admin/debug endpoints registered alongside public routes with no separation.
func SetupRouter() *gin.Engine {
	r := gin.Default()

	// Public routes
	r.GET("/api/v1/products", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"products": []string{}})
	})
	r.GET("/api/v1/products/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id")})
	})
	r.POST("/api/v1/orders", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"status": "created"})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: admin and debug endpoints mixed directly into public router
	r.GET("/admin/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"users": []string{}})
	})
	r.DELETE("/admin/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"deleted": true})
	})
	r.GET("/admin/config", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"config": map[string]string{}})
	})
	r.GET("/debug/pprof", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"profile": "pprof"})
	})
	r.GET("/debug/vars", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"vars": map[string]int{}})
	})

	return r
}
